note: Sprint 5.17/5.18 hotfix (5.18c): Trinity payment path address regex aligned with canonical SOST encoding. The canonical address format produced by sost-cli newwallet / getnewaddress is `sost1` + 40 lowercase hex chars (45 chars total), as defined in include/sost/address.h. Earlier Trinity sprints used a bech32-shaped regex `^sost1[023456789acdefghjklmnpqrstuvwxyz]{20,80}$` which rejected legitimate real addresses generated by the operator on the VPS (e.g. sost10a262c66d8fdcb45bd21b073b7d8674dac542542, which contains 1 and b — both excluded from bech32). The new regex is `^sost1[0-9a-f]{40}$`, with the canonical pattern centralised as the module-level constant SOST_ADDRESS_PATTERN in scripts/trinity/useful_compute_worker_address_map.py. Updated files: scripts/trinity/{useful_compute_worker_address_map.py, useful_compute_payment_proposal.py, useful_compute_payment_draft.py}, schemas/trinity/{useful_compute_payment_draft.schema.json, useful_compute_payment_proposal.schema.json}, and every test fixture that used the old bech32-shaped fakes. New tests/trinity/test_useful_compute_address_format.py covers the three real addresses, the truncated-by-one-char case, uppercase rejection, wrong-prefix rejection, embedded-space rejection, the lingering 'q' ornament and the template placeholder. No consensus, RPC, wallet, signer or wire-format changes.
